introSection: Aurora Flying Coaster (Simplified Chinese: 超速极光过山车) is a steel flying coaster located at Silk Road Paradise in Qindu, Xianyang, Shaanxi, China manufactured by Chinese firm Jinma Rides. The ride's track shares similarities with B&M track and the load station is comparable to the Vekoma Flying Dutchman product.

sections.title: Elements
sections.content:
* Chain Lift Hill
 * Lie-to-Fly
 * Pretzel Loop
 * In-Line Twist
 * Helix
 * Tunnel
 * Fly-to-Lie

sections.title: Ride Experience
sections.content: Once boarded, the train seats lower to the horizontal position. After dispatching, the ride begins with an 180-degree turnaround and a lift hill. The lift hill is high. At the top is a pre-drop and a half-inversion, a Lie-to-Fly. The ride next makes a 90-degree turn into a Pretzel Loop. Next, riders makes a 90-degree-banked right handed turn into an In-Line Twist. Riders then go to the left and bank to the right, entering some helixes. Next the train enters a Tunnel, a Fly-to-Lie, and then the final brake run.

fullText:
Aurora Flying Coaster (Simplified Chinese: 超速极光过山车) is a steel flying coaster located at Silk Road Paradise in Qindu, Xianyang, Shaanxi, China manufactured by Chinese firm Jinma Rides. The ride's track shares similarities with B&M track and the load station is comparable to the Vekoma Flying Dutchman product.




 * Chain Lift Hill
 * Lie-to-Fly
 * Pretzel Loop
 * In-Line Twist
 * Helix
 * Tunnel
 * Fly-to-Lie

Once boarded, the train seats lower to the horizontal position. After dispatching, the ride begins with an 180-degree turnaround and a lift hill. The lift hill is high. At the top is a pre-drop and a half-inversion, a Lie-to-Fly. The ride next makes a 90-degree turn into a Pretzel Loop. Next, riders makes a 90-degree-banked right handed turn into an In-Line Twist. Riders then go to the left and bank to the right, entering some helixes. Next the train enters a Tunnel, a Fly-to-Lie, and then the final brake run.
